Title: Bernard Arnault, a 'free-marketeer' who whispers in the ear of every president
Subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (2/6). The LVMH chief has never hidden his allegiance to France's pro-business, establishment right. He's close to fellow billionaire Vincent Bolloré and, above all, pragmatic. Now he's preparing for the far right's rise to power.

US launches new strikes against Iran after Trump warns country will pay for deaths of American soldiers
Subhead: The United States launched a fresh round of attacks against Iran late on Monday in the tenth consecutive night of attacks. On Tuesday morning, Iran's Revolutionary guard said it had hit US military targets in Bahrain and Kuwait.

Title: Between China and the US, a new diplomacy of detainees emerges
Subhead: Alongside the major issues discussed between the world's two leading powers, the fate of American citizens imprisoned in China on espionage charges has also become a bargaining chip.

Title: Climate change: Why the ECB refuses to create a lower interest rate for green investments
Subhead: China's central bank guarantees low-cost funding for green investments. Its European counterpart, although a frontrunner on climate change, currently refuses to use this tool.

Title: Repeated heatwaves fuel eco-anxiety and anger among youth: 'It feels like we're living in a dystopia'
Subhead: In the wake of the third heatwave to hit France since May, frustration and anxiety have gripped part of the younger generations, faced with what some call 'the coolest summer of the rest of their lives.'

Subhead: The coach stepped down after a dramatic 6-4 loss in the World Cup third-place match against England on Saturday. Over his 14 years in the role, he nonetheless managed to restore France to the pinnacle of world football.

Title: 20 men, a 'sperm party,' and two days of sexual torment: The brutality of the 'libertine' rapes case
Subhead: Over the course of a long weekend, one of Christophe B.'s former partners was forced to endure sexual assaults by numerous men at their home. Some of these assaults were broadcast live on the swinging website Wyylde.

Title: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
Subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.
